rulings.Case Title: MARTHA DEAN VS. MORE THAN CLOSETS INC DBA CALIFORIA CLOSETS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 9, 2007, LINE 6 DEFENDANT MORE THAN CLOSETS INC DBA CALIFORIA CLOSETS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. 1) DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. 2) DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S CAUSE OF ACTION FOR HARASSMENT. THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT SUPPORT A FINDING OF SEVERE AND PERVASIVE CONDUCT; RENO V, BAIRD (1998) 18 CAL.4TH 640. 3) DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT'S REASONS FOR TERMINATION ARE PRETEXTUAL. THE FACTS INCLUDE; (a)WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAD PERFORMANCE PROBLEMS BEFORE AND AFTER MS.ZUTTER OR MR. FOLEY WERE HIRED. (b)WHETHER PLAINTIFF RECEIVED ADEQUATE TRAINING TO BE ABLE TO EFFECTIVELY USE THE MARKETWARE SOFTWARE, A PROGRAM THE DEFENDANT CEASED TO USE. (c)THE EXPLANATION TAHT MS. YOSHEN'S DISPERATE TREATMENT WAS DUE TO A DISABLITY ACCOMODATION, AN ACCOMODATION DISPUTED BY MS.YOSHEN. (d)THE DESIRE TO STAFF THE NEW SHOWROOM WITH YOUNGER EMPLOYEES. (e)THE REASONABLE INFERENCE THAT MS. YOSHEN TOOK PLAINTIFF'S POSTION BECAUSE OF MS. YOSHEN'S YOUTH AND APPEARANCE. COLLECTIVELY, A REASONABLE INFERENCE FROM THESE AND RELATED FACTS IS THAT PLAINTIFF'S TERMINATION WAS DUE TO HER AGE AND THAT THE PERFORMANCE REVIEW AND PROBATION STATUS THAT WERE TIED TO UTILIZATION OF THE MARKETWARE SOFTWARE WERE PRETEXTUAL. =(302/PJM/PB)
